title: Largest Square of Ones
description:
Given a binary matrix of '0' and '1', find the area of the largest square containing only '1's.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ m, n ≤ 300
explanation:
Intuition
dp[r][c] is the side length of the largest square whose bottom-right corner is (r,c). If matrix[r][c]==1, dp[r][c]=1+min(top,left,diag).

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize dp with zeros
2. Main Algorithm: Scan cells; when see '1', compute from three neighbors; track max side
3. Return Result: Return max_side^2

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(mn)
- Space Complexity: O(mn) or O(n) rolling

Key Insights
- Local rule grows squares only from three neighbors
- Area requires squaring side length

Common Pitfalls
- Treat chars as ints consistently
- Borders have fewer neighbors

testCases.explanation: When maximal_square(matrix=[[0]]), the expected result is 0
